description: Phase 2 / Item 3 of the coherence refactoring: introduce a data-driven prompt assembler (PromptContext + SectionSpec fn-pointer roster + assemble()) that BOTH the sequential and slot/wave prompt-composition paths consume, retiring the hand-enforced 'any new section MUST also be wired through slot' rule in prompt/mod.rs. Spike-validated design in docs/designs/prompt-assembler-contract.md (approach C: fn-pointer spec table + roster-completeness test). Each path supplies its OWN independently-ordered roster (set-subset, not order-subset); parity is proven per-section AND per-path whole-prompt.

criteria: Rust: No warnings in `cargo check` output
Rust: No warnings in `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` output
Rust: All scoped tests pass with `cargo test -p task-mgr <scope>`
No breaking changes to the public re-exports loop_engine::prompt::{build_prompt, BuildPromptParams, PromptResult} or slot::SlotPromptBundle's Send contract unless explicitly required by the task
The `assert_impl_all!(SlotPromptBundle: Send)` assertion in tests/prompt_slot.rs still compiles after every change
globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks

priorityPhilosophy.description: Hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN — Anticipate edge cases before coding
2. PHASE 2 FOUNDATION — ~1 day now to save ~2+ weeks later (1:10+ ratio); we are pre-launch, foundations compound
3. FUNCTIONING CODE — Pragmatic, reliable, wired in per plan
4. CORRECTNESS — Compiles, type-checks, scoped tests pass deterministically
5. CODE QUALITY — Clean code, qualityDimensions satisfied, no warnings
principles: Quality dimensions explicit — qualityDimensions on every task tells you what 'good' looks like
Phase 2 foundation — prefer solutions that lay strong post-launch foundations (1:10+ savings ratio)
Edge cases = test cases — every known edge case must have a corresponding test
Scoped per-iteration tests, full suite at REVIEW-001 — REVIEW must leave the trunk green including pre-existing failures
Byte-identical migration — each section migrated one (group) at a time behind a parity test; per-path whole-prompt parity, since each path keeps its own roster order

prohibitedOutcomes: Tests that only assert 'no crash' or check type without verifying the rendered prompt bytes
Tests that mirror implementation internals (break when refactoring)
A section whose text is still inlined in sequential.rs/slot.rs after it has been migrated (two sources of truth)
Sorting/emitting sections by SectionKind (criticals-first) instead of each path's roster order — changes whole-prompt byte layout silently
Trying to make ONE shared roster reproduce both paths' whole-prompt byte layouts — the two paths have different display orders; each path supplies its own ordered roster
Forgetting to clear shown_learning_ids when the learnings section is dropped — silently skews the UCB bandit
Threading per-section budget through PromptContext instead of SectionKind — collapses per-section budgets into one shared field
Letting the sequential overflow contract regress: assemble() reporting dropped=["CRITICAL"] must still surface as Err(PromptOverflow) to the five-rung ladder

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: docs/designs/prompt-assembler-contract.md §CONTRACT-001 interface block is present and every referenced type resolves in the crate (grep-verified: Connection, Task, PermissionMode, Path, PathBuf — and confirm there is NO `SiblingPrd`/`SynergyCluster` type; the real inputs are batch_sibling_prds: &[PathBuf], run_id, task_prefix)

Positive: the invariants are documented — single render site; per-path roster ordering (slot ⊂ sequential as a SET, independently ordered); critical-overflow translation to Err(PromptOverflow) in the sequential caller; learnings side-output centralized; TOTAL_PROMPT_BUDGET parity
Positive: known-bad discriminators (phase/kind-order emit, missing id-clear, collapsed budgets, one-roster-for-both-paths) are documented
Positive: downstream-impact list maps to FEAT-001..FEAT-007 here
Negative: no production source file is modified by this task

Known-bad: a 'verification' that only confirms the file exists (not that the types resolve) would pass a naive check but miss a stale signature — must grep each referenced type, and must confirm SiblingPrd/SynergyCluster are NOT referenced
userStories.description:
Design-only verification gate. The contract is authored in docs/designs/prompt-assembler-contract.md (spike result 2026-05-23, approach C; corrected after PRD review on 2026-05-23 to use real section signatures). This task confirms the interface is complete and unambiguous before downstream FEATs implement against it.

DO: Read docs/designs/prompt-assembler-contract.md §CONTRACT-001 in full. Verify each type referenced by the PromptContext / SectionKind / Rendered / SectionSpec / Assembled / assemble() interface resolves in the crate (grep to confirm). Verify the invariants (including per-path roster ordering and the sequential critical-overflow translation), known-bad discriminators, edge cases, and downstream-impact list are present and map to FEAT-001..FEAT-007.
DO NOT: Write any production code. Do NOT redesign the contract. If a genuine signature gap is found (the phantom-type bug was already fixed), record it in the progress log and note it on the affected FEAT.

Edge cases:
- A referenced type doesn't exist / has a different signature: record the delta in the progress log so the affected FEAT uses the real one.
edgeCases: Referenced type signature drift: record delta, do not silently proceed
userStories.notes: Contract authored by /spike 2026-05-23 (decision B), corrected after md-to-json PRD review same day (phantom SiblingPrd/SynergyCluster removed; per-path roster + overflow-translation invariants added). Full text in docs/designs/prompt-assembler-contract.md. Learning 3940: !Send fear unfounded. Verify signatures against: src/loop_engine/prompt/core.rs (build_learnings_block), src/loop_engine/prompt_sections/{dependencies,task_ops,siblings,synergy}.rs. Note synergy is a permanent no-op (build_synergy_section returns String::new()).
qualityDimensions: The contract is stable enough that seven independent FEATs can implement against it without revision
Every type named in the interface block resolves to a real crate type with the stated signature
userStories.title: Prompt assembler section contract (PromptSection + PromptAssembler)

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: assembler.rs defines PromptContext, SectionKind, Rendered, SectionSpec, Assembled, assemble() exactly per docs/designs/prompt-assembler-contract.md §CONTRACT-001 (real types only)
Positive: const _: fn() = || { fn assert_send<T: Send>() {} assert_send::<SectionSpec>(); }; compiles (SectionSpec: Send)
Unit test: assemble() with three synthetic specs (2 critical + 1 trimmable) emits text in ROSTER order regardless of which phase rendered them
Unit test: criticals exceeding total_budget → Assembled.prompt == "" and dropped_sections == ["CRITICAL"]
Unit test: a trimmable that doesn't fit is named in dropped_sections; a section rendering "" is NOT named in dropped_sections
Unit test: when a synthetic 'learnings'-kind section carrying shown_learning_ids is dropped, Assembled.shown_learning_ids is empty
Negative: Assembled contains no &Connection / no non-Send field
Known-bad: a roster where the assembler sorts by SectionKind (criticals first) before emitting would pass a presence test — the order test above catches it
CONTRACT: try_fit_section reused from existing module (grep to confirm the symbol path), not reimplemented
userStories.description:
Create src/loop_engine/prompt/assembler.rs implementing the CONTRACT-001 interface (approach C, fn-pointer spec table). No real section is migrated yet — this is the engine + types + the assemble() loop, unit-tested in isolation with 2-3 synthetic SectionSpecs.

DO: Define PromptContext<'a> (closed set of main-thread-available borrows, per the corrected contract — no phantom types), SectionKind { Critical, Trimmable { budget } }, Rendered { text, shown_learning_ids }, SectionSpec { name, kind, render: fn(&PromptContext, SectionKind) -> Rendered }, Assembled { prompt, section_sizes, dropped_sections, shown_learning_ids }, and `pub fn assemble(ctx, roster, total_budget) -> Assembled`. Reuse the existing try_fit_section budget logic (learning 2663) verbatim inside the Phase-2 trimmable loop. Render criticals first for the budget gate, store keyed by name, EMIT in roster order. Add a const Send assertion on SectionSpec.
DO NOT: Migrate any real section. Do NOT store &Connection in Assembled. Do NOT collapse per-section budgets into a PromptContext field — budget rides on SectionKind::Trimmable. Do NOT sort the roster by kind before emitting.

Edge cases:
- criticals alone exceed total_budget → return Assembled with empty prompt + dropped_sections == ["CRITICAL"] (the uniform overflow signal; callers translate per the contract).
- a render fn returns "" → NOT pushed into dropped_sections.
- learnings section dropped → assemble() clears its shown_learning_ids.
- empty roster → empty prompt, no panic.
edgeCases: criticals exceed budget → empty prompt + dropped_sections == ["CRITICAL"]
render fn returns empty string → not counted as dropped
learnings section dropped → shown_learning_ids cleared
empty roster → empty prompt, empty section_sizes, no panic

failureModes.expectedBehavior: return empty prompt + dropped == ["CRITICAL"] (uniform signal; caller translates)

failureModes.cause: trimmable section over its SectionKind budget
failureModes.expectedBehavior: drop it, record name in dropped_sections, continue

userStories.notes: New file src/loop_engine/prompt/assembler.rs; register `pub mod assembler;` in prompt/mod.rs. Reuse try_fit_section (learning 2663 — grep src/loop_engine/prompt/core.rs and prompt_sections/mod.rs for its location). The load-bearing Send assertion is assert_impl_all!(SlotPromptBundle: Send) in tests/prompt_slot.rs — preserve it; the new SectionSpec: Send assertion is cheap insurance. TOTAL_PROMPT_BUDGET=80_000. The uniform dropped=["CRITICAL"] signal is translated by callers (FEAT-003), not by assemble().
qualityDimensions: assemble() is the single emit site; render-phase ordering is internal and never leaks into emit order
Budget accounting is byte-for-byte the existing try_fit_section logic, not a reimplementation
No heap/dyn dispatch (approach C) — specs are plain structs with fn pointers
userStories.title: Assembler skeleton: PromptContext + SectionSpec + assemble()

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: the dependencies section is produced by exactly one render fn reached via each path's assembler roster
Positive: a sequential roster and a slot roster exist as independently-ordered Vec<SectionSpec>; the dependencies spec sits at its legacy position in each
Positive: assert_impl_all!(SlotPromptBundle: Send) still compiles; slot still builds its bundle on the main thread before thread::spawn
Parity test: Rendered.text for the dependencies spec == legacy build_dependency_section(conn, &task.id) for (a) a task with 2+ deps and (b) a task with zero deps — byte-identical
Negative: the dependencies section text is NOT inlined anywhere in sequential.rs/slot.rs after this change
Negative: no &Connection crosses a thread boundary (grep slot.rs spawn site)
Known-bad: a parity test comparing against a literal expected string (not the legacy fn) would pass even if the legacy fn changed — test MUST diff against the live legacy fn output
CONTRACT: build_dependency_section signature matches the real one in src/loop_engine/prompt_sections/dependencies.rs (grep to verify args)
userStories.description:
Migrate ONE section — `dependencies` (trimmable + needs &Connection, present in BOTH the sequential and slot rosters) — to a SectionSpec rendered via assemble(). Prove byte-identical output for that section in both paths, and establish the per-path roster pattern.

DO: Wrap build_dependency_section(conn, &task.id) in a SectionSpec render fn. Introduce a sequential roster and a slot roster, each as its OWN ordered Vec<SectionSpec> preserving that path's existing display order; place the dependencies spec at its current position in each. Wire each path to call assemble() for (at least) the dependencies section while the not-yet-migrated sections remain inlined around it. Add tests/prompt_assembler_parity.rs asserting (a) Rendered.text == legacy build_dependency_section output, and (b) the section appears at the correct position in each path's roster.
DO NOT: Migrate any other section. Do NOT change the dependency-section text. Do NOT read &Connection on a worker thread — slot builds the bundle on the main thread before spawn. Do NOT attempt one shared roster for both paths.

Edge cases:
- task with no dependencies → section renders empty, parity holds.
- task with deps that span multiple statuses → text matches legacy exactly.

Known-bad to guard against:
- 'parity' test that compares against a hand-written expected string rather than the live legacy builder output — must call the legacy fn and diff.
edgeCases: task with zero dependencies → empty section, parity holds
task with deps in mixed statuses → byte-identical to legacy

failureModes.cause: assemble() section output differs from legacy by whitespace/order
failureModes.expectedBehavior: parity test fails; fix the render fn until byte-identical

userStories.notes: Pilot section = dependencies (BOTH rosters, trimmable + conn). Legacy fn: build_dependency_section(conn, &task.id) in src/loop_engine/prompt_sections/dependencies.rs (call sites: sequential.rs ~line 288, slot.rs ~line 280). Send assertion to preserve: assert_impl_all!(SlotPromptBundle: Send) in tests/prompt_slot.rs:38. iteration_pipeline_parity.rs is a STRUCTURAL reference for parity-test shape only — the new byte-diff test in tests/prompt_assembler_parity.rs is greenfield. Keep legacy fn callable until FEAT-007 deletes the wiring rule. Learning 2705: slot historically drifted — parity test is the guard.
qualityDimensions: Byte-identical parity proven by diffing against the live legacy builder, not a frozen string
Each path's roster preserves that path's own legacy display order
Slot path keeps all &Connection access on the main thread
userStories.title: Pilot-migrate the dependencies section through the assembler (both paths) + byte-parity test

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: task, task_ops, completion, base_prompt render via Critical SectionSpecs in both paths' rosters at their legacy positions
Positive: sequential build_prompt returns Err(TaskMgrError::PromptOverflow) when assemble() reports dropped_sections == ["CRITICAL"] — verified by a unit test that forces criticals over budget
Positive: slot keeps the sentinel-in-bundle overflow behavior — verified by a unit test
Parity test (per section): assemble() section text == legacy helper output, byte-identical, per path
Negative: none of these sections' text remains inlined in sequential.rs/slot.rs

Known-bad: returning the sentinel (empty prompt) from sequential WITHOUT translating to Err(PromptOverflow) would silently break overflow::handle_prompt_too_long's ladder — the forced-overflow test catches it
CONTRACT: TaskMgrError::PromptOverflow variant + overflow::handle_prompt_too_long exist and are unchanged (grep src/loop_engine/overflow.rs)
userStories.description:
Migrate the SectionKind::Critical sections shared by both paths — the task envelope, task_ops, completion, and base_prompt — to SectionSpecs, each behind a per-path parity assertion. Critically: wire the sequential caller to translate assemble()'s uniform dropped=["CRITICAL"] signal back into Err(TaskMgrError::PromptOverflow) so the five-rung overflow ladder is unchanged; the slot caller keeps its sentinel-in-bundle behavior.

DO: Add a SectionSpec (kind=Critical) per section wrapping the existing helper. Place each at its legacy position in BOTH rosters. In sequential build_prompt, when assemble() returns dropped_sections == ["CRITICAL"], return Err(TaskMgrError::PromptOverflow{..}) exactly as today (sequential.rs ~238-244). In slot, keep the sentinel-in-bundle behavior (slot.rs ~215-235). Add a test for each overflow contract.
DO NOT: Migrate trimmables (learnings/source/etc.) — later tasks. Do NOT change any section text. Do NOT unify the two overflow contracts — they legitimately differ at the caller.

Edge cases:
- criticals overflow in sequential → Err(PromptOverflow), ladder engages.
- criticals overflow in slot → sentinel-in-bundle, unchanged.
- missing base prompt file → empty section, warn, continue (preserve current behavior).
edgeCases: sequential criticals overflow → Err(PromptOverflow)
slot criticals overflow → sentinel-in-bundle
missing base prompt file → empty section + warn, no panic

failureModes.cause: sequential returns empty prompt instead of Err on critical overflow
failureModes.expectedBehavior: forced-overflow test fails; translate dropped==["CRITICAL"] to Err(PromptOverflow)

userStories.notes: Criticals trio + base_prompt. The two overflow contracts: sequential returns Err(PromptOverflow) (sequential.rs ~238-244) feeding overflow::handle_prompt_too_long's 5-rung ladder (learnings 2852/2031); slot uses sentinel-in-bundle (slot.rs ~215-235). assemble() signals both uniformly via dropped=["CRITICAL"]; the translation lives in each caller. Display orders differ per path — place each spec at its own legacy index.
qualityDimensions: Sequential overflow contract (Err(PromptOverflow)) preserved so the 5-rung ladder is byte-unchanged
Slot sentinel contract preserved
One render site per critical section; no inlined critical text remains
userStories.title: Migrate the critical sections (task, task_ops, completion, base_prompt) + sequential overflow translation

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: learnings renders via a Trimmable SectionSpec in both paths' rosters at legacy positions
Positive: shown_learning_ids clear-on-drop lives ONLY in assemble() — grep confirms it's removed from sequential.rs and slot.rs (slot.rs ~262-269)
Unit test: learnings section dropped under budget → resulting Assembled.shown_learning_ids is empty (centralized invariant)
Parity test: learnings section text == legacy output per path (sequential = recall-limit-driven bytes; slot = LEARNINGS_BUDGET=4000 bytes)
Negative: learnings text not inlined in sequential.rs/slot.rs; no duplicate id-clear remains
Known-bad: forgetting to remove the slot-side clear (leaving it in addition to assemble()'s) would double-clear and pass a naive test — the grep-for-removed-clear AC catches it
CONTRACT: core::build_learnings_block(conn, task, budget) signature matches the real one (grep src/loop_engine/prompt/core.rs)
userStories.description:
Migrate the `learnings` section (Trimmable) to a SectionSpec, and centralize the side-output invariant: when learnings is dropped under budget pressure, assemble() (already, from FEAT-001) clears shown_learning_ids — so the per-builder clears in sequential.rs/slot.rs must be REMOVED, leaving assemble() as the sole owner. This is the highest-risk section (UCB-skew known-bad).

DO: Wrap core::build_learnings_block(conn, task, budget) in a Trimmable SectionSpec carrying its own budget on SectionKind::Trimmable{budget}. Remove the now-duplicated shown_learning_ids clear from slot.rs (~262-269) and any sequential equivalent. Parity-test the learnings section per path. NOTE: the sequential learnings path is recall-limit-driven (it does NOT use a LEARNINGS_BUDGET const the way slot.rs:37 does) — 'parity' for sequential learnings means matching the recall-limit-driven output, not a 4000-byte truncation; carry whatever budget reproduces each path's current bytes.
DO NOT: Leave any shown_learning_ids clearing logic in the path builders. Do NOT collapse the learnings budget with the source budget.

Edge cases:
- learnings trimmed under budget → assemble() clears shown_learning_ids (no per-builder clear).
- no learnings to show → empty section, empty shown_learning_ids, parity holds.
edgeCases: learnings trimmed → shown_learning_ids cleared centrally
no learnings → empty section + empty ids, parity holds

failureModes.cause: a builder still clears shown_learning_ids locally
failureModes.expectedBehavior: remove it; assemble() owns the invariant — grep test catches the duplicate

userStories.notes: Highest-risk section — the shown_learning_ids side-output feeds the UCB bandit; a missed/duplicated clear silently skews ranking. assemble() owns the clear (FEAT-001); this task DELETES the per-builder clears (slot.rs ~262-269). Budget asymmetry (P3-2 from review): LEARNINGS_BUDGET=4000 exists only in slot.rs:37; sequential learnings is recall-limit-driven via build_learnings_section — match each path's actual bytes, not a single 4000 truncation. Per-section budgets stay distinct from SOURCE_CONTEXT_BUDGET.
qualityDimensions: shown_learning_ids invariant centralized in assemble(), zero duplication in path builders
Per-path learnings byte parity (recall-limit-driven sequential vs 4000-byte slot) preserved
Learnings budget distinct from source budget
userStories.title: Migrate the learnings section + centralize the shown_learning_ids side-output invariant

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: source, steering, session_guidance, tool_awareness, key_decision each render via a SectionSpec in both paths' rosters at legacy positions
Parity test (per section): assemble() output == legacy helper output, byte-identical, per path
Unit test: SOURCE_CONTEXT_BUDGET (2000) remains distinct — a source overflow does not shrink the learnings budget and vice versa
Negative: none of these sections' text remains inlined in sequential.rs/slot.rs
Failure mode: missing base/steering file → empty section + warning, no panic (matches pre-migration)
Known-bad: collapsing SOURCE_CONTEXT_BUDGET and LEARNINGS_BUDGET into one shared field passes a single-section test but fails the distinct-budget test
CONTRACT: SOURCE_CONTEXT_BUDGET const exists in both sequential.rs:42 and slot.rs:34 with value 2000 (grep to confirm before unifying onto SectionKind::Trimmable)
userStories.description:
Migrate the remaining sections shared by both paths — source/source-context, steering, session_guidance, tool_awareness, key_decision — each as a SectionSpec at its legacy position in both rosters, each behind a per-path parity assertion. After this task, every SHARED section flows through the assembler; only sequential-only sections (FEAT-006) remain inlined.

DO: Add a SectionSpec per section (Critical or Trimmable as the legacy code treats it — source is Trimmable{SOURCE_CONTEXT_BUDGET=2000}; steering/session_guidance/tool_awareness/key_decision per current behavior). Wrap the existing core/prompt_sections helpers. Parity-test each per path. Keep SOURCE_CONTEXT_BUDGET distinct from the learnings budget.
DO NOT: Touch sequential-only sections (synergy/escalation/siblings/reorder) — FEAT-006. Do NOT change section text. Do NOT collapse budgets.

Edge cases:
- empty task_files → source section empty, not an error.
- missing steering file → empty section, warn, continue.
- no key decision → empty section, parity holds.
edgeCases: empty task_files → empty source section
missing steering file → empty section + warn
no key decision → empty section

failureModes.expectedBehavior: distinct-budget test fails; keep budget on each SectionKind::Trimmable

userStories.notes: Remaining shared trimmables/criticals. SOURCE_CONTEXT_BUDGET=2000 (sequential.rs:42, slot.rs:34). Helpers in core.rs + prompt_sections. Learning 2664: extract duplicated builder logic to core — the helpers become render-fn bodies. After this task every shared section is assembler-driven; FEAT-006 handles sequential-only.
qualityDimensions: One render site per section; zero inlined shared-section text remains
Per-section budgets preserved distinctly via SectionKind::Trimmable{budget}
Each section migrated behind its own per-path parity assertion
userStories.title: Migrate the remaining shared trimmables (source, steering, session_guidance, tool_awareness, key_decision)

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: synergy, escalation, siblings, reorder render via SectionSpecs present in the sequential roster only, at legacy positions
Positive: the slot roster does NOT contain these specs (assert on the slot roster construction)
Parity test: each sequential-only section's assemble() output == legacy builder output, byte-identical (synergy parity == empty string)
Unit test: when reorder_hint is None / batch_sibling_prds is empty, the corresponding spec is absent/empty rather than rendering a stray artifact
Negative: no sequential-only section text remains inlined in sequential.rs
Negative: the slot prompt's byte output is unchanged by this task

CONTRACT: build_sibling_prd_section takes (conn, task_id, task_prefix, batch_sibling_prds: &[PathBuf]) and build_synergy_section takes (conn, task_id, run_id) and returns String::new() — grep src/loop_engine/prompt_sections/{siblings,synergy}.rs to verify
userStories.description:
Migrate the sections that ONLY appear in the sequential roster — synergy, escalation, siblings, reorder-hint — as SectionSpecs present in the sequential roster but absent from the slot roster. This proves the per-path roster set-subset (slot ⊂ sequential) works with independently-ordered Vecs.

DO: Add SectionSpecs for synergy/escalation/siblings/reorder using their REAL helper signatures (build_synergy_section(conn, task_id, run_id) — a permanent no-op returning String::new(); build_sibling_prd_section(conn, task_id, task_prefix, batch_sibling_prds: &[PathBuf]); escalation + reorder per current code). Put them in the sequential roster only at their legacy positions. Confirm the slot roster legitimately omits them. Parity-test each in the sequential path.
DO NOT: Add these to the slot roster. Do NOT invent SiblingPrd/SynergyCluster types — use &[PathBuf] / the real args. Do NOT change section text. Do NOT 'fix' the synergy no-op — migrate it as-is behind a parity test.

Edge cases:
- reorder hint absent (None) → spec omitted from the sequential roster for that task.
- batch_sibling_prds empty → siblings section empty, parity holds.
- synergy → always empty (no-op), parity = empty string.
edgeCases: reorder_hint None → spec omitted
batch_sibling_prds empty → siblings empty
synergy → always empty (no-op)

failureModes.cause: a sequential-only spec leaks into the slot roster

userStories.notes: Sequential-only sections prove slot's roster ⊂ sequential's as a SET (contract residual risk #1). Real signatures (NOT phantom types): build_sibling_prd_section(conn, task_id, task_prefix, batch_sibling_prds: &[PathBuf]) in siblings.rs:41 (private SiblingPrdSummary at :24); build_synergy_section(conn, task_id, run_id) in synergy.rs — permanent no-op returning String::new(). PromptContext carries run_id/task_prefix/batch_sibling_prds/reorder_hint as Option; slot leaves them None.
qualityDimensions: Per-path rosters are plain ordered data; the SAME SectionSpec type serves both — no path-specific spec variants
Absent optional inputs omit/empty the spec rather than rendering a stray section
Slot byte output provably unchanged

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: the 'must also be wired into slot / no second source of truth' comment is gone from prompt/mod.rs, replaced by an assembler-pointer note
Positive: the duplicate 'must also be wired through the wave builder' rule in src/loop_engine/CLAUDE.md is also removed/replaced (grep CLAUDE.md confirms no surviving copy)
Positive: a roster-completeness test asserts every known section NAME appears in the sequential roster (roster vs enumerated known-set, NOT a hardcoded count) and fails naming the missing section
Positive: docs/designs/coherence-refactoring.md §3 updated to reflect the assembler and mark Item 3 complete

Now that every section flows through the shared assembler, retire the 'any new section MUST also be wired into slot — there is no second source of truth' rule, in BOTH places it lives, and replace it with a roster-completeness test (recovering approach-B exhaustiveness as a test).

DO: Remove/replace the wiring-rule doc comment in prompt/mod.rs (~lines 17-19). ALSO remove/replace the duplicate rule in src/loop_engine/CLAUDE.md (the 'Prompt-builder companion … sections added to the sequential prompt MUST also be wired through the wave builder' note). Add a test (in tests/prompt_assembler_parity.rs or a new completeness test) asserting every known section NAME appears in the sequential roster, comparing the roster against the enumerated known-section set (NOT a hardcoded count), failing with a message naming the missing section. Update docs/designs/coherence-refactoring.md §3 to mark Item 3 done and describe the assembler.
DO NOT: Leave the old 'wire through slot' instruction anywhere (grep both prompt/mod.rs and CLAUDE.md). Do NOT remove the SlotPromptBundle Send assertion.

Edge cases:
- a section registered in slot but missing from sequential → completeness test flags (sequential is the superset roster).
- a brand-new unregistered section name → test fails naming the missing section.
edgeCases: section in slot roster but absent from sequential → flagged
new unregistered section → test fails naming it

failureModes.cause: a future section added without a SectionSpec
failureModes.expectedBehavior: completeness test fails in CI naming the missing section

userStories.notes: Payoff task — removes the manual discipline the whole refactor retires. The rule lives in TWO places: src/loop_engine/prompt/mod.rs (rustdoc //! ~lines 17-19) AND src/loop_engine/CLAUDE.md ('Prompt-builder companion'). Delete/replace BOTH. Completeness-test approach: enumerate known section names, assert each is in the sequential roster; fail naming the missing one. Update coherence-refactoring.md §3 in the same change.
qualityDimensions: The manual 'remember to wire slot too' discipline is fully replaced by a CI-enforced completeness test
Both copies of the retired rule are gone (prompt/mod.rs AND CLAUDE.md)
Test failure messages name the offending section, not a count
userStories.title: Delete the hand-enforced wiring rule (both copies) + add roster-completeness test

acceptanceCriteria: No code duplication across render fns that a shared helper would eliminate (DRY)
assemble() and roster builders are single-responsibility and as small as practical
Roster construction for sequential vs slot shares the common spec definitions (rosters differ only in membership + order)
Code follows existing prompt-module patterns (core helpers as render-fn bodies)
Any issues found spawn REFACTOR-FIX-xxx tasks via `task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by REVIEW-001`
userStories.description:
Review the assembler + all migrated sections for DRY violations, complexity, coupling, and clarity.

DO: Check for duplicated render-fn boilerplate that could share a helper, assemble() complexity, roster-construction duplication between the sequential and slot rosters, and whether SectionKind/SectionSpec stay as simple as the contract intends.
DO NOT: Re-architect away from approach C. Do NOT skip.
